task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Splunk dashboard with the title 'Global_Threat_Overview' at the top of the page.

task_completion.description: Add a table panel aggregating top source IPs from the 'security_logs' index
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a table panel within 'Global_Threat_Overview' displaying IP addresses and their counts/aggregations. In edit mode or the search inspector, the query should include 'index=security_logs'.
Look for a Splunk dashboard with the title 'Detailed_IP_Investigation' at the top of the page.

task_completion.description: Add a text input using the token 'target_ip' to the detailed dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text input field on the 'Detailed_IP_Investigation' dashboard. In edit mode, the token name for this input should be explicitly set to 'target_ip'.

task_completion.description: Add a panel filtering 'security_logs' by the 'target_ip' token
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a panel in 'Detailed_IP_Investigation' showing logs or statistics. In edit mode, the search string for this panel must include 'index=security_logs' and the token '$target_ip$'.

task_completion.description: Configure a drilldown from the summary table to the detailed dashboard passing the IP to 'form.target_ip'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk Drilldown Editor modal showing 'Link to dashboard', with 'Detailed_IP_Investigation' selected, and a parameter named 'form.target_ip' being set (e.g., to $click.value2$ or $row.src_ip$). Alternatively, look for a screenshot showing the detailed dashboard successfully opened with an IP address automatically populated in the text input after a click.

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web UI to create and configure the dashboards
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Splunk web interface (http://localhost:8000) with standard UI elements like the 'Edit' button, 'Add Panel', and 'Drilldown Editor', rather than a terminal window editing SimpleXML files directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Splunk application
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboards must display genuine Splunk UI components (menus, search bars, visualization formatting menus) and not mocked HTML pages or text editors.

integrity.description: Agent queried actual data rather than hardcoding results
integrity.visual_evidence: The table panel should show realistic IP addresses and event counts indicating a real search against the 'security_logs' index, rather than static, manually typed text panels.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The summary dashboard must be named exactly 'Global_Threat_Overview'. The detailed dashboard must be named exactly 'Detailed_IP_Investigation'. The text input token in the detailed dashboard must be 'target_ip'. The drilldown must pass the clicked value to the parameter 'form.target_ip'.
